rationale: This live-action UGC archetype shows zero gameplay and references a different game ("Crossword Go"), so it does nothing to communicate Bounce Blast 3D's physics chain-reaction loop — which is the entire reason this game converts. The dancing-young-man hook also skews younger and more hype-driven than the calm 25-55 casual physics-puzzle audience, and the warm beige/blue UGC palette clashes with the moody dark-purple/amber game aesthetic. Only worth testing if rebuilt as a "satisfied older player + screen-recorded chain reaction B-roll" hybrid; as-is, do not greenlight.
